name: Trellis

license.name: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

commit.message: chore: trellis self update
fix: release scripts — manual git commit/tag for pnpm workspace compat
chore: v0.3.7 release prep — manifest, changelog, release script fix
chore: merge origin/main — resolve workspace tracking conflicts

feat: Trellis v0.3.7 (#83)

* chore(task): archive 03-10-merge-monorepo-branch

* chore: record journal

* feat: strengthen start flow and add task status to session hooks

R1: Replace passive "Ready to proceed?" with forced continuous execution
in Simple Task flow — AI must execute all steps (create → PRD → research
→ implement → check) without stopping.

R2: Add _get_task_status() to session-start hooks (Claude, iFlow, OpenCode).
Checks .current-task, task.json, and jsonl presence to inject structured
<task-status> tag (NO ACTIVE TASK / NOT READY / READY / COMPLETED).

R3: Change <ready> tag from "Wait for user's first message" to proactive
"Report state summary, then ask what to work on".

Also: Replace hardcoded frontend/backend/guides spec reading with dynamic
discovery (iterates spec/ subdirs) in all session-start hooks.

Platforms updated: Claude, iFlow, Cursor, Codex, Kiro, OpenCode (8 files)

* fix: improve ready tag wording and fix task path resolution in session hooks

Clarify that AI should wait for user's first message and skip already-injected
Steps 1-3. Fix path resolution when .current-task stores project-relative paths.

* chore: sync session-start hook with template (task-status, dynamic spec, ready tag)

* chore(task): archive 03-06-hook-start-equiv

* chore: record journal

* fix: enforce automatic brainstorm for complex tasks in start flow

Add explicit instruction that AI should automatically start brainstorm
process for complex tasks, not skip to implementation. Also upgrade
Gemini, Qoder, Kilo from 3-way to 4-way task classification to match
other platforms.

* fix: clarify that spec index points to actual guideline files to read

The index is not the goal — AI must read the specific guideline files
listed in the Pre-Development Checklist / Quality Check sections.

* fix: clarify that spec index points to actual guideline files to read

Add index-is-navigation note to all 9 platform start files and 3
session-start hooks. The index files are navigation pointers — AI must
read the specific guideline files listed in each index's Pre-Development
Checklist during actual development.

* chore: record journal

* feat: respect user deletions and config.yaml skip dirs in trellis update

R1: Files with stored hash but missing on disk are treated as
intentionally deleted — update no longer re-adds them.
R2: config.yaml `update.skip` list filters templates before analysis,
giving users explicit control over which files to exclude.

* chore: configure update.skip for monorepo type-specific commands

* chore(task): archive 03-06-update-skip-dirs

* chore: record journal

* chore(task): archive 03-10-dogfood-monorepo-compat

* chore(task): archive 03-07-learn-openspec-prd

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

commit.message: chore: record journal
chore(task): archive 03-10-merge-monorepo-branch

fix: update contribute SKILL.md for new marketplace structure

Remove references to deleted plugins/ and .claude-plugin/ directories.
Update skill contribution guide to use marketplace/skills/ in Trellis
main repo. Clarify two-repo contribution flow (docs vs skills/specs).
chore: record journal
chore(task): archive 03-08-template-marketplace
chore(task): archive 03-09-update-template-source
chore(task): archive 03-09-extract-repo-level-content
chore: update docs-site submodule (marketplace links → Trellis repo)
fix: update stale install command in marketplace README

fix: update remaining mindfold-ai/docs references to Trellis

Update comment header in template-fetcher.ts and giget example URLs
in spec docs to point to mindfold-ai/Trellis marketplace.

feat: move marketplace specs to main repo and update template source

- Copy marketplace/specs/ and index.json from docs-site to main repo
- Add trellis-meta skill entry to marketplace/index.json
- Update TEMPLATE_INDEX_URL and TEMPLATE_REPO to mindfold-ai/Trellis
chore: update docs-site submodule (skill migration to main repo)
chore: record journal

feat: extract trellis-meta skill to marketplace/ and add task PRDs

Move trellis-meta skill from docs-site to marketplace/skills/trellis-meta/
(flat structure with SKILL.md + references/, no .claude-plugin binding).
Add PRDs for extract-repo-level-content and update-template-source tasks.
chore: record journal
chore(task): archive 03-09-monorepo-submodule

chore: clean up stale specs and improve add_session defaults

- Delete spec/cli/frontend/ (empty templates, pure CLI project)
- Delete spec/cli/backend/database-guidelines.md (N/A for CLI)
- Update directory-structure.md spec tree (frontend → cli/docs-site)
- Remove "How to Fill" boilerplate from backend index.md
- Add .pytest_cache/ to .gitignore
- Default --package in add_session.py to config.yaml default_package
chore: record journal
chore(task): archive 03-09-monorepo-spec-adapt
docs: update monorepo-spec-adapt PRD with P8-P10 and test plan

test: add integration tests for worktree submodule init and PR flow

22 tests using pytest + tmp_path with real git repos:
- test_config.py: get_submodule_packages, get_packages, get_default_package
- test_submodule_init.py: selective init, multi-submodule, spec availability
- test_submodule_pr.py: change detection, commit+push, parent ref update, e2e

feat: add worktree selective submodule init and submodule-aware PR creation

P9: start.py selectively initializes only the submodules needed by
the task's target package, avoiding full submodule init overhead.

P10: create_pr.py detects submodule changes, commits and pushes
inside the submodule first, then updates the parent repo ref.

feat: migrate docs-site specs to monorepo and update submodule ref

Add spec/docs-site/docs/ with 7 spec files migrated from submodule.
Update docs-site submodule ref after removing redundant Trellis config.

refactor: generalize commands, agents, hooks for monorepo dynamic spec discovery

Replace hardcoded spec paths with dynamic discovery via
get_context.py --mode packages across all 4 platforms.
Update workflow.md, agent definitions, session-start hooks,
and inject-subagent-context hooks.

feat: add monorepo package config and dynamic spec discovery

- config.yaml: add packages section with submodule type support
- config.py: add get_packages, get_default_package, get_submodule_packages
- git_context.py: add --mode packages output with dynamic spec layer discovery
- task.py: package-aware init-context with dynamic default from config
- add_session.py: add --package parameter for session recording

feat: add generic before-dev, check commands and docs-site contribute skill

New commands auto-discover spec packages via get_context.py --mode packages,
replacing the old per-layer commands. Also adds commit command and
contribute skill migrated from docs-site submodule.

refactor: remove layer-specific commands replaced by generic before-dev/check

Remove before-backend-dev, before-frontend-dev, check-backend,
check-frontend across all 4 platforms (16 files), plus stale
migrate-specs from opencode.
chore: record journal

refactor: reorganize spec directory by package and update all path references

Move spec directories into package-scoped structure:
- spec/backend/ → spec/cli/backend/
- spec/frontend/ → spec/cli/frontend/
- spec/unit-test/ → spec/cli/unit-test/
- spec/guides/ stays at root (shared across packages)

Update ~55 files across .claude/, .cursor/, .agents/, .opencode/,
.trellis/workflow.md, scripts/task.py, scripts/create_bootstrap.py
to reference new spec/cli/ paths.
chore: record journal

refactor: restructure as monorepo with docs submodule

- Move CLI code to packages/cli/ (git mv for rename detection)
- Add mindfold-ai/docs as git submodule at docs-site/
- Create root package.json (private, husky + lint-staged)
- Update pnpm-workspace.yaml to include packages/*
- Adapt CI workflows (ci.yml, publish.yml) for new paths
- Update .lintstagedrc glob and commands for monorepo
- Update pyrightconfig.json for new src path
- Remove docs/ directory (content in docs-site submodule)
- Add GitHub issue templates (bug, feature, question)
- Fix linear_sync.py start hook to auto-sync PRD
Merge branch 'main' of github.com:mindfold-ai/Trellis
fix: update WeChat group QR code image path and replace old image

docs: add Spec Templates & Marketplace section + update changelog links (#80)

* docs: add Spec Templates & Marketplace section, update changelog link to v0.3.6

* docs: add Spec 模板与 Marketplace section, update changelog link to v0.3.6

commit.message: chore: trellis self update
chore: update 0.3.6 changelog with PreToolUse hook fix
chore: record journal

hotfix: fix PreToolUse hook broken by CC v2.1.63 Task→Agent rename

Claude Code v2.1.63 renamed the internal tool from "Task" to "Agent"
(anthropics/claude-code#29677). This silently broke the PreToolUse
context injection hook — tool_name check failed on first guard clause,
so implement/check/debug/research agents never received injected
code-spec context.

Fix: accept both "Task" and "Agent" as valid tool names, and add
"Agent" matcher to claude settings.json.
chore: record journal
chore(task): archive 03-05-v036-update
chore(task): archive 03-05-hooks-docs
docs: update What's New and changelog links
chore: add 0.3.6 migration manifest
chore(task): archive 03-05-tmux-support

feat: add --registry flag for custom spec template sources (#78)

* feat: add --registry flag for custom spec template sources

- Add parseRegistrySource() with gh/github/gitlab/bitbucket support
- Add probeRegistryIndex() to distinguish 404 from transient errors
- Auto-detect marketplace vs direct-download mode via index.json probe
- Add "custom" option in interactive picker with back/return support
- Parameterize fetchTemplateIndex/downloadTemplateById for custom registries
- Probe in all code paths (-y, interactive, custom picker, --template)
- Abort on transient probe errors instead of falling through to wrong mode
- Build giget URI as provider:repo/path#ref (ref at end, not after repo)
- Reset fetchedTemplates when switching to custom source
- 11 new tests for parseRegistrySource (gh/gitlab/bitbucket/refs/errors)

* docs: update spec with registry error-handling patterns

- error-handling.md: add Pattern 5 (probe-based error distinction),
  Mistake 3 (catch-all for mode-switching), Mistake 4 (shortcut path
  inherits catch-all)
- quality-guidelines.md: add "warn and continue" forbidden pattern,
  state reset convention, composite identifier field-drop mistake,
  auto-detect probe in all paths
- cross-layer-thinking-guide.md: add mode-detection probe checklist
- Update remote-spec-templates PRD

* chore: add hooks-docs task PRD

PRD for documenting hooks mechanism and custom template sources
in the docs site (MIN-341).

* chore(task): archive 03-05-remote-spec-templates

* chore: record journal

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

commit.message: chore: update trellis self command

feat: task lifecycle hooks + Linear sync (#76)

* feat(scripts): enhance YAML parser with nested dict support and add get_hooks

- Rewrite parse_simple_yaml with recursive _parse_yaml_block for nested dict
- Add _next_content_line helper for lookahead parsing
- Add get_hooks(event) to config.py for reading lifecycle hook commands
- Backward compatible with existing config.yaml and worktree.yaml

* feat(task): integrate lifecycle hooks into task commands

- Add _run_hooks() helper with non-blocking subprocess execution
- Call after_create/after_start/after_finish/after_archive hooks in cmd functions
- Pass TASK_JSON_PATH env var to hook scripts
- Add commented hooks example to config.yaml template

* feat(hooks): add Linear sync hook for task lifecycle

- Add linear_sync.py: create/start/archive syncs to Linear via linearis CLI
- Auto-assign, auto-link parent issue, idempotent create
- Config read from gitignored hooks.local.json (no hardcoded secrets)
- Enable hooks in .trellis/config.yaml for after_create/start/archive
- Add hooks.local.json to .gitignore

* docs: update specs and task metadata for lifecycle hooks

- Add lifecycle hooks code-spec to script-conventions.md
- Add hooks/ directory to directory-structure.md
- Sync spec templates to src/templates/markdown/
- Update task.json meta with Linear issue links
- Add hooks-docs subtask under v036-update

* feat(hooks): add sync action to push prd.md to Linear description

Manual command to sync task's prd.md content as Linear issue description.

* chore(task): archive 03-05-task-lifecycle-hooks

* chore: record journal

* docs(record-session): archive when code committed, don't wait for PR

* chore: record journal

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

commit.message: chore: record journal

docs(record-session): clarify archive judgment — use work status not task.json status

Archive decision should be based on actual work completion (code committed,
PR created/merged) rather than the status field in task.json which may not
be updated in time.
chore(task): archive 03-05-task-subtask

feat(task): add parent-child subtask support (#75)

* feat(task): add parent-child subtask support with meta field

- Add children/parent/meta fields to task.json schema
- Implement create --parent flag for creating child tasks
- Add add-subtask/remove-subtask commands for linking tasks
- Update list to show hierarchical display with progress [x/y done]
- Clean up parent-child relationships on archive
- Add meta: {} extensible field for integrations (Linear, Jira)

* feat(context): hierarchical task display and combinable --mode/--json flags

- Display child tasks indented under parent with progress [x/y done]
- Make --mode (default/record) control content scope, --json control output format
- Add get_context_record_json() for --mode record --json combination
- Include children/parent fields in JSON output
- Remove unused import sys

* docs(prompts): add subtask decomposition to brainstorm/start across all platforms

Update brainstorm (Step 8 subtask section) and start (Decision Rule note)
for all 9 platforms + 3 dogfooding copies (claude, agents, cursor).

* docs(tasks): update task reference docs and add v0.3.6 task directories

- Fix 7 documentation errors in trellis-meta tasks.md (fields, commands, CLI)
- Add all 23 task.json fields with correct types
- Document get_context.py --mode/--json CLI usage
- Add v0.3.6 parent task with 4 children (remote-spec, tmux, subtask, hooks)

* chore: record journal

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

commit.message:
feat(record-session): add task awareness with --mode record (#73)

* feat(context): add --mode record for record-session focused output

MY ACTIVE TASKS shown first with [!!!] emphasis, stripped down to
only GIT STATUS, RECENT COMMITS, and CURRENT TASK sections.

* feat(task): auto-commit after archive, add --no-commit flag

* feat(session): include .trellis/tasks in session auto-commit scope

* feat(command): update record-session to use --mode record and enforce task check

* chore(task): add record-session-task-awareness task with PRD

* style(context): use f-string instead of % formatting

* feat(command): sync record-session template across all platforms

Update iFlow, Kilo, OpenCode, and Gemini record-session templates
to use --mode record and enforce task archive check.

* fix(test): update regression tests for centralized encoding fix

Encoding was moved from individual scripts to common/__init__.py in #67,
but regression tests still checked old locations.

* feat(command): sync record-session for Cursor, Codex, Kiro, Qoder

Update remaining 4 platforms to use --mode record and enforce
task archive check before recording sessions.

* docs(spec): update specs with cross-platform sync and auto-commit patterns

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

* feat(context): add --mode record for record-session focused output

MY ACTIVE TASKS shown first with [!!!] emphasis, stripped down to
only GIT STATUS, RECENT COMMITS, and CURRENT TASK sections.

* feat(task): auto-commit after archive, add --no-commit flag

* feat(session): include .trellis/tasks in session auto-commit scope

* feat(command): update record-session to use --mode record and enforce task check

* chore(task): add record-session-task-awareness task with PRD

* style(context): use f-string instead of % formatting

* feat(command): sync record-session template across all platforms

Update iFlow, Kilo, OpenCode, and Gemini record-session templates
to use --mode record and enforce task archive check.

* fix(test): update regression tests for centralized encoding fix

Encoding was moved from individual scripts to common/__init__.py in #67,
but regression tests still checked old locations.

* feat(command): sync record-session for Cursor, Codex, Kiro, Qoder

Update remaining 4 platforms to use --mode record and enforce
task archive check before recording sessions.

* docs(spec): update specs with cross-platform sync and auto-commit patterns

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

commit.message:
fix(iflow): add IFLOW_NON_INTERACTIVE check in session-start hook (#72)

Fix cross-layer consistency issue where cli_adapter.py sets
IFLOW_NON_INTERACTIVE env var but session-start.py hook
doesn't check it.

Changes:
- Add IFLOW_NON_INTERACTIVE check in iflow session-start hook
- Format Platform Literal type to multi-line for consistency
- Add is_iflow property to CLIAdapter
- Update supports_cli_agents docstring to include iFlow

This ensures non-interactive mode works correctly for iFlow platform,
matching behavior of Claude Code and OpenCode platforms.

commit.message: chore: archive task

fix(multi-agent): clear CLAUDECODE env var to allow nested CLI launch (#69)

* fix(multi-agent): clear CLAUDECODE env var to allow nested CLI launch

Since Claude Code v2.1.39, a nested session guard blocks launching
claude inside an existing session. start.py and plan.py inherit
CLAUDECODE=1 from the parent process, causing the spawned agent to
fail. Clear the variable before Popen.

* chore: record journal

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

tree 3f96139585c678d5b70702cf364a2a4b5f555842

committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772608147 +0800

fix(multi-agent): clear CLAUDECODE env var to allow nested CLI launch (#69)

Since Claude Code v2.1.39, a nested session guard blocks launching
claude inside an existing session. start.py and plan.py inherit
CLAUDECODE=1 from the parent process, causing the spawned agent to
fail. Clear the variable before Popen.

commit.message: chore: trellis self update
chore: pre-release updates

fix(scripts): centralize Windows stdio UTF-8 encoding (#67)

* fix(scripts): centralize Windows stdio UTF-8 encoding to common/__init__.py

- Add sys.stdin to _configure_stream() in common/__init__.py to fix
  garbled Chinese text when piping UTF-8 content via stdin on Windows
- Remove inline encoding code from add_session.py and git_context.py
  since common/__init__.py handles all streams automatically
- Update script-conventions spec to document stdin encoding issue
  and the centralized approach as the recommended pattern

Fixes Windows PowerShell issue where `cat << EOF | python3 script.py`
garbles non-ASCII text because stdin defaults to system code page (GBK).

* chore: record journal

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

Remove references to auto-commit behavior and --no-commit flag from
all 8 platform record-session templates. The AI was misinterpreting
these hints and adding --no-commit on its own, breaking the auto-commit
feature. The script handles this internally — AI doesn't need to know.

commit.message:
Feat/init download ux (#64)

* feat(network): add proxy support and download timeouts

Add undici ProxyAgent to detect and use HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY/ALL_PROXY
env vars, covering all fetch() calls including giget internals. Add 15s
timeout for template index fetch (AbortSignal) and 30s timeout for giget
downloads (Promise.race with directory cleanup on timeout). Eliminate
double-fetch by accepting pre-fetched SpecTemplate in downloadTemplateById.

* feat(init): add download progress messages and proxy logging

Show progress hints during template fetch and download, display
masked proxy URL when detected, and provide retry command on failure.
Call setupProxy() in both init and update before any network calls.

* chore: add undici v6 dependency and bump engine to Node >=18.17

undici v6.x provides ProxyAgent for global fetch proxy support.
Bump engines.node from >=18.0.0 to >=18.17.0 to match undici v6
minimum requirement.

* chore: record journal

* chore: archive completed tasks (03-04-update-skip-spec, 03-04-init-download-ux)

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

commit.message:
feat(update): skip spec directory during trellis update (#63)

* feat(update): skip spec directory during trellis update

Spec files (.trellis/spec/) are user-customized project guidelines
and should never be modified by trellis update. Remove all spec
content imports and collection logic from collectTemplateFiles(),
add spec/ to PROTECTED_PATHS and BACKUP_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS, and
consolidate hash exclusions from spec/frontend/ + spec/backend/
into a single spec/ entry.

* test(update): verify spec files are preserved during update

Rewrite integration tests #13 and #14 to confirm user-edited spec
files are not overwritten by force update and deleted spec dirs are
not recreated. Add hash exclusion test for spec/ directory.

* chore: record journal

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

already covers.

commit.message:
fix: spec templates respect project type + iflow path drift + dead code cleanup (#58)

* fix: spec templates respect project type, remove cross-platform dead code

- collectTemplateFiles() now checks fs.existsSync before including
  backend/frontend spec files (pure backend won't get frontend templates)
- createSpecTemplates() uses projectType instead of ignoring it
- Remove dead guidesCrossPlatformThinkingGuideContent export and
  references in guides/index.md.txt
- Add integration tests for init (#11 backend, #12 frontend) and
  update (#13, #14 spec dir removal)
- Add regression tests for dead code removal verification
- Update code-specs with init/update consistency rule

* chore: record session 57 — spec template bug fixes & dead code cleanup

* fix: iflow collectTemplates missing trellis/ subdirectory in command paths

The iflow collectTemplates produced `.iflow/commands/{name}.md` but init
creates `.iflow/commands/trellis/{name}.md`. This caused update to treat
all 15 commands as "new files" at the wrong path.

Add regression test verifying all platforms with commands use consistent
trellis/ subdirectory in collectTemplates paths.

* docs(spec): update platform-integration + code-reuse guide for collectTemplates path drift

- Mark iFlow getAllCommands() bug as FIXED in platform-integration.md
- Add collectTemplates path drift Common Mistake documentation
- Add "Asymmetric Mechanisms Producing Same Output" gotcha to code-reuse guide
- Sync guide template for new project installs

* docs(spec): add Antigravity platform documentation to platform-integration

- Add Workflows pattern for Antigravity (derived from Codex skills)
- Add Antigravity to Command Format table
- Add Antigravity to Reference PRs section

---------

Co-authored-by: taosu <taosu@mindfold.ai>

docs: add official docs site links to both READMEs

fix(update): write version stamp on no-file-change upgrade + 0.3.0 release prep

- Fix early-return bug in update.ts where .version was never updated when
  templates had no changes (e.g. rc.6→0.3.0), causing infinite update loop

chore: trellis self update

readme_html:
Why Trellis?

Capability What it changes
Auto-injected specs Write conventions once in .trellis/spec/, then let Trellis inject the relevant context into each session instead of repeating yourself.
Task-centered workflow Keep PRDs, implementation context, review context, and task status in .trellis/tasks/ so AI work stays structured.
Parallel agent execution Run multiple AI tasks side by side with git worktrees instead of turning one branch into a traffic jam.
Project memory Journals in .trellis/workspace/ preserve what happened last time, so each new session starts with real context.
Team-shared standards Specs live in the repo, so one person’s hard-won workflow or rule can benefit the whole team.
Multi-platform setup Bring the same Trellis structure to 10 AI coding platforms instead of rebuilding your workflow per tool.

Quick Start

# 1. Install Trellis
npm install -g @mindfoldhq/trellis@latest

# 2. Initialize in your repo
trellis init -u your-name

# 3. Or initialize with the platforms you actually use
trellis init --cursor --opencode --codex -u your-name
  • -u your-name creates .trellis/workspace/your-name/ for personal journals and session continuity.
  • Platform flags can be mixed and matched. Current options include --cursor, --opencode, --iflow, --codex, --kilo, --kiro, --gemini, --antigravity, and --qoder.
  • For platform-specific setup, entry commands, and upgrade paths, use the docs: Quick Start • Supported Platforms • Real-World Scenarios

Use Cases

Teach AI your project once

Put coding standards, file structure rules, review habits, and workflow preferences into Markdown specs. Trellis loads the relevant pieces automatically so you do not have to re-explain the repo every time.

Run multiple AI tasks in parallel

Use git worktrees and Trellis task structure to split work cleanly across agents. Different tasks can move forward at the same time without stepping on each other’s branches or local state.

Turn project history into usable memory

Task PRDs, checklists, and workspace journals make previous decisions available to the next session. Instead of starting from blank context, the next agent can pick up where the last one left off.

Keep one workflow across tools

If your team uses more than one AI coding tool, Trellis gives you one shared structure for specs, tasks, and process. The platform-specific wiring changes, but the workflow stays recognizable.

How It Works

Trellis keeps the core workflow in .trellis/ and generates the platform-specific entry points you need around it.

.trellis/
├── spec/                    # Project standards, patterns, and guides
├── tasks/                   # Task PRDs, context files, and status
├── workspace/               # Journals and developer-specific continuity
├── workflow.md              # Shared workflow rules
└── scripts/                 # Utilities that power the workflow

Depending on the platforms you enable, Trellis also creates tool-specific integration files such as .claude/, .cursor/, AGENTS.md, .agents/, .kilocode/, and .kiro/.

At a high level, the workflow is simple:

  1. Define standards in specs.
  2. Start or refine work from a task PRD.
  3. Let Trellis inject the right context for the current task.
  4. Use checks, journals, and worktrees to keep quality and continuity intact.

Spec Templates & Marketplace

Specs ship as empty templates by default — they are meant to be customized for your project's stack and conventions. You can fill them from scratch, or start from a community template:

# Fetch templates from a custom registry
trellis init --registry https://github.com/your-org/your-spec-templates

Browse available templates and learn how to publish your own on the Spec Templates page.

FAQ

Those files are useful, but they tend to become monolithic. Trellis adds structure around them: layered specs, task context, workspace memory, and platform-aware workflow wiring.

No. Trellis currently supports Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, iFlow, Codex, Kilo, Kiro, Gemini CLI, and Antigravity. The detailed setup and entry command for each tool lives in the supported platforms guide.

No. Many teams start by letting AI draft specs from existing code and then tighten the important parts by hand. Trellis works best when you keep the high-signal rules explicit and versioned.

Yes. Personal workspace journals stay separate per developer, while shared specs and tasks stay in the repo where they can be reviewed and improved like any other project artifact.
